objective: Persist Blog representative cover metadata at write time so public Blog reads use stored columns without cache or load-test target shortcuts.
changed: Added coverAssetId to Blog create requests and commands.
Added omitted-vs-explicit-null Blog update semantics for coverAssetId.
Added IBlogCommandStore.GetAssetPublicUrlsAsync with an Assets.AsNoTracking projection.
Blog create/update handlers write CoverAssetId and PublicCoverUrl from Assets.PublicUrl at save time.
Added admin integration coverage for create, preserve-on-omit, change, and clear cover behavior.
Added a Postgres contract proving public Blog detail uses one measured command and stored PublicCoverUrl.
intentionallyNotChanged: No Real Backend target selection change.
No public/admin body split in this slice.
No Docker runtime package change for the pre-existing libgssapi startup log.
goalVerification: Blog cover resolution is now a write-time operation for admin create/update.
Public Blog reads continue to project stored PublicCoverUrl.
Public Blog detail has one-command Postgres contract coverage.
Omitted coverAssetId preserves existing cover; explicit null clears it.
Load targets remain current public slugs and pageSize=12.

validations.command: diff guard for pageSize/seed/load target changes
validations.result: passed; no production target semantic change
passed: Component 122, Unit 56, Architecture 35, Integration 213, Contract 1 skipped

validations.command: docker compose 2cpu total8gb overlay rebuild and health probe

validations.result: passed: 9001 requests, 300.01 rps, failed_rate 0, dropped_iterations 0, p95 4.10 ms

risks: This is not the body payload split; large detail serialization remains a likely next bottleneck.
The local k6 run is a 300 rps sanity check, not a 1000 rps spike proof.
The local database is not the exact production content corpus.
Backend Alpine runtime still logs missing libgssapi_krb5.so.2 at startup.
MediatR license warning remains in the production-environment local compose run.
recommendation: Ship this slice, then continue public/admin body model split, app serialization timing, DB command/open p95 availability, and k6 p99 export before considering cache.
